data.epi_content:
After finishing the kiss on the back of my hand and meeting his eyes, it feels more embarrassing than I expected.

At times like this, I'm grateful for my nearly expressionless face.

It means I can hide how flustered I actually am.

"By the way, Evan, shall we go for a walk?

My body's fine now, and I can see clearly, so let's explore around here."

Evan nodded.

I wonder if he grew up in an environment where speaking wasn't necessary.

I used to mutter to myself like a madwoman just so I wouldn't forget the sound of speech.

What kind of environment doesn't require speaking?

Perhaps one where you receive countless barbed words from others, making it unnecessary to speak yourself.

When he saved me, he must have killed all those people who were around me.

I open the door and leave the Dormitory.

As I walk, I fall deep into thought.

It's not that killing is wrong.

If necessary, if someone must be killed, then killing is the right thing to do—at least for that individual.

But is it really necessary to kill people now?

Just look at Vivian—judging by the magic she uses, she could kill a thousand people like me with a flick of her finger, but she doesn't. It's either some kind of moral principle, or the certainty that someone capable of killing her will eventually come along.

Now I just want to live comfortably in one place, neither bound by anyone nor running away.

"Let's go to the bench by the pond."

I walk to the bench and sit down.

Evan naturally sits right beside me.

Yet he constantly scans our surroundings for threats, like a deserter who's been endlessly hunted.

The stupid-looking carp that should be swimming in circles in the pond has stopped circling and is now resting against the wall.

It almost looks like it's trying to dig through the bottom to escape somewhere.

"Evan, take that carp out and throw it into the river nearby."

"Why the carp all of a sudden?"

"Just because."

Evan places the carp in a bubble of water, lifts it into the air, and begins moving it somewhere.

I just suggested it casually—I wasn't sure if he could actually do it.

No matter how stupid that carp looks, I doubt it enjoys swimming in circles in that pond all day.

That's why I asked him to release it.

"Evan, what are you planning to do now?"

Instead of answering immediately, Evan walks around the bench and begins chanting some spell.

A semi-transparent barrier forms around us, eventually becoming completely invisible.

"The Crown Prince will have to be dealt with somehow.

I can't kill him now.

But that bastard is probably still trying to kill you somehow."

"..."

It was difficult to respond to that.

By the way, is it really that easy to talk about dealing with the Crown Prince?

I wouldn't know since I've never accomplished anything I've tried to do.

No matter how many shots I fired, the Crown Prince wouldn't die.

"Evan, um.

This is a bit awkward to ask directly, but do you think you're objectively strong?"

At that, Evan shakes his head.

But as he does, I catch a glimpse of a slightly chilling smile on his face.
data.epi_content2:
"At least right now, I'm weak.

Give me a month, no, even a week, and I think I could match Vivian."

"But Vivian is a genius."

"How many years do you think I've lived?

Erica, after bringing you back here from being half-dead, I lived three more years.

And in the other times when you died, three years is nothing..."

He suddenly stops speaking.

"I just had to learn magic somehow. And that required time."

Evan might be older than I thought.

If you add up all the time he's lived, he could be old enough to be called a grandfather.

Indeed, there's something in his every movement that suggests years of experience.

When I die, I leave behind the world I lived in, but those who remain don't.

But there's no need to overthink it.

He probably hasn't spent meaningful time developing personally.

Neither have I.

Still, I casually throw out a joke.

"Should I call you 'elder' then?"

Evan becomes serious at that.

"Not like that."

"I don't know what you're thinking, but all I want is a cozy room, a cup of tea, and occasionally some nice snacks and a cigarette."

Evan seems slightly taken aback by my words.

Of course, after experiencing such trauma, it would be natural to be consumed by hatred and resentment.

I am filled with anger, but before that, I'm afraid.

Afraid of failing, of suffering the same fate again and not coming back alive this time.

Especially for Evan's sake.

What Evan is suggesting means turning Vivian into an enemy.

Vivian won't forgive immorality.

The beloved female protagonist of this world won't tolerate people like Evan and me.

"Don't you get angry when you see the Emperor, no, the Crown Prince?

Don't you get angry seeing that stupid me, or that vile Lydia?"

When I say this, Evan asks me back, somewhat dejectedly or perhaps angrily.

"Angry... of course I am.

Why wouldn't I hate them?

Those damn Blacks who treated me like livestock, Johannes who imprisoned me and trampled my limbs, eyes, and dignity, Lydia who tormented me as soon as our family fell.

I hate them all terribly, yes. But."

I paused for a breath while speaking.

This was essentially a confession of sorts.

It was especially difficult to say to Evan, who had saved me.

"I'm scared. Terribly scared.

If I resist, who knows what horrible things they might do to me.

And they might do the same to you.

What if I come back again, and you—the you who cares about me now—are gone, and all that's left is a you who wants to tear me apart with words? I'd go insane.

Honestly, I've been desperately holding onto my sanity until now!

Sometimes I did lose it, but that was because it was too hard."

It's difficult to say these things while sober.

If only I could blow these words away with cigarette smoke, or say them with the slight warmth of alcohol.

When I say such things while sober, I become aware of how weak and useless I am.
data.epi_content3:
I want to maintain an inflated sense of self, but it's not working.

"I'm a coward, a scaredy-cat, and selfish, so I can't make a choice.

Right now... right now, I'm happy. I'm alive, I'm not in pain, and there's someone who cares about me.

So I'm scared. Scared that everything will suddenly go back to how it was."

At that, Evan snickers and flicks my forehead.

I was honestly pouring my heart out, but his response felt somewhat mocking, which made me feel a bit hurt.

Though I'm not in a position to complain.

Evan splashes water from the pond and begins shining light on it.

Soon, images start appearing above the water droplets.

"If you can't make a choice, I'll help you.

Erica, you just need to say the word. Whatever you want, I'll make it all come true.

I told you, didn't I? That I'd protect you. There's no need to be afraid at all."

And in the images that appear, people are seen dying in agony.

Soldiers clutching spears and trembling violently begin to wither like skeletons in an instant.

Eventually, they turn to dust. Thousands of people, it seems.

And then the Crown Prince appears, tied to a pole and slowly burning to death.

It's never happened in this world, but it's quite a pleasant sight.

"Are you still afraid of Johannes, that idiot who can't do anything but hide behind others?"

I nodded.

Of course I'm afraid, after what happened to me.

"Vivian is strong, of course, but we'll manage somehow.

I've fought her many times. Many, many times.

I don't always win either. But in the end, I'm the one who came back!"

The screen showing people withering to death scatters, and Evan stands up and faces me.

He looks into my eyes and smiles reassuringly.

"There's nothing to worry about.

There's no need to be afraid.

Erica, just say the word.

Even if your soul is mixed with something else, I don't ask questions.

In the end, your essence remains the same.

Rather, if it hadn't been mixed, you would have collapsed, so maybe you should be grateful."

Then he approaches me, brings his face close, and presses his forehead against mine, just like when he sent me back here.

He's already cast a soundproofing spell, so no one but me can hear him, yet he whispers in my ear.

I wonder how he noticed that my soul is mixed.

It doesn't matter. If he wanted to separate it, he should have done so before my neck was cut in the square.

"Don't hesitate.

If you want to kill the Crown Prince, tie him to a pole and burn him to death.

Put a gun to the foreheads of those you hate and pull the trigger."

They say that when one becomes a Black Magic user, they offer sacrifices to evil gods and have various conversations.

It sounds just like a demon tempting a person.

As if saying, "Sell your soul, and I'll grant whatever you desire..."

I imagine Johannes trembling in fear before me, rubbing his hands together and begging for his life.

I imagine capturing those who mistreated me and tormenting them cruelly.

I imagine carrying Vivian around like a doll.

Suddenly, the corners of my mouth rise and refuse to come down.

Whether it's joy, tension, or fear, I don't know, but my breathing becomes slightly rapid.

My breathing becomes a bit faster, and my expressionless face breaks into a happy smile.

Seeing my expression, Evan says:

"I'll be right behind you."

Our smiles were both distorted.
